rationale: In the theory section, the cited paper is used in a claim-specific argument that many same-name SKUs can overwhelm shoppers and reduce perceived variety, which is directly tied to the citing paper's focal assortment-elasticity question. This is more than a generic background mention because it helps justify an expected mechanism the citing paper later tests and discusses. The citing study does not reuse Briesch et al.'s model or data, but it does rely on its empirical insight to frame this part of its conceptual setup.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Briesch et al. (2009) as prior empirical support that assortment characteristics can shape store choice and perceived variety. It uses that support to motivate and interpret its prediction that assortment-size effectiveness may weaken after private-label umbrella rebranding.
